_comment: Commercial Domain Ontology Schema — node labels and edge types for bounded context model
_source: Confluence PC space Domain Entity Model pages

nodeLabels.name: BoundedContext
nodeLabels.description: A DDD bounded context representing a cohesive domain model with clear boundaries. Bounded contexts group related entities and define ownership, upstream/downstream relationships, and integration patterns.
nodeLabels.usage_example: BC1 Product Catalogue, BC4 Pricing & Markdown, BC6 Assortment & Range

nodeLabels.name: Entity
nodeLabels.description: A domain entity representing a core business concept with identity and lifecycle. Entities have attributes, relationships, and business rules. They are the building blocks of the domain model.
nodeLabels.usage_example: Item, Supplier, Purchase Order, Promotion, Allocation

edgeTypes.name: PART_OF
edgeTypes.description: Indicates that a node is a component or member of a larger aggregate or collection. Used for entity-to-bounded-context relationships and hierarchical structures.
edgeTypes.usage_example: Entity Item is PART_OF BoundedContext BC1 Product Catalogue
